core_mechanism: endowing a space of state parameters with a Riemannian metric taken from the Hessian of a potential function, then reading its scalar curvature as a diagnostic of interaction strength that diverges at critical/limiting points
brief_reason: The abstract uses the Ruppeiner scalar (the curvature of the Hessian metric built on the thermodynamic potential's state variables) as a measure of underlying interactions and notes it diverges at extremal limits, which is exactly the Hessian-metric/scalar-curvature diagnostic machinery.
